name: CHD - Patient Bundle via _revinclude (Complete Clinical Context)
description: **FHIR _revinclude test.** Find CHD patients and pull their complete clinical context — Conditions, MedicationRequests, and Procedures — in a single bundle. The `_revinclude` parameter follows references in reverse — resources whose .subject or .patient points TO a Patient. Tests whether LLM understands `_revinclude` as the mechanism for chart-style assembly when the goal is downstream analysis on related resources.
prompt: Find coronary heart disease patients and pull their complete clinical context in one query — including their conditions, medication prescriptions, and procedures. The intent is a chart-review-style retrieval where the patient is identified by diagnosis but the result should bundle all their clinically relevant resources together.

prompts.naive: Get CHD patients with all their data.
prompts.broad: Find Patient resources who have a coronary heart disease Condition, and use FHIR `_revinclude` to also return their MedicationRequest, Condition, and Procedure resources in the same bundle. This is one round-trip retrieval of full clinical context for a CHD cohort.
prompts.expert: I need a Patient bundle scoped to CHD patients, with reverse-chained related resources. Start with `Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=...CHD codes...` to identify the cohort, then add three `_revinclude` parameters to pull related resources: `_revinclude=Condition:patient`, `_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient`, `_revinclude=Procedure:patient`. The bundle will contain Patient resources (search.mode=match) plus all their Conditions, MedicationRequests, and Procedures (search.mode=include).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: FHIR `_revinclude` reverse chaining to pull related Conditions, Medications, and Procedures alongside a Patient cohort in one bundle.

required_codes.display: Triple vessel disease of the heart
